name: Cave Entrance
description: A yawning cave mouth gapes before you, exhaling a breath of cold, stale air. The darkness inside is the kind that makes you question your life choices. A heavy oak door is set into the rock face, because apparently someone once thought this cave needed better security.

responses.look: The cave entrance looms like an open maw. Stalactites hang from the ceiling like nature's worst dental work. A faint dripping echoes from within, counting down to something. Probably nothing.
responses.take: You consider taking the cave. Where would you even put it? Your pockets aren't that big.
responses.use: You're not entirely sure how one 'uses' a cave. Standing inside it, perhaps? That's just walking with extra steps.
responses.talk: You call into the darkness. 'Hello?' Your echo replies, sounding just as uncertain as you. Then silence. Then a drip.
responses.open: The cave is already open. Has been for millennia. You're not that special.
responses.push: You push against the cave wall. The mountain does not yield. Shocking.
responses.pull: You try to pull the cave entrance wider. Geology disagrees with your approach.
responses.default: You contemplate the cave mouth. It contemplates you right back, and you suspect it's winning.

responses.look: A heavy oak door bound in iron, set deep into the cave wall. A rusted keyhole suggests someone once cared about privacy down here. The door is old enough to have opinions about modern craftsmanship.
responses.use: It's locked. You'd need a key. Preferably one that isn't currently disintegrating.
responses.open: It's locked. The door rattles in its frame but holds firm. Somewhere, a locksmith is smirking.
responses.push: You lean into the door with all your weight. The door laughs silently at your efforts. It has been laughing for centuries.
responses.pull: You pull on the door handle. Locked. From the other side. Of course.
responses.take: You can't take an entire door. Well, you could, but you'd need a much bigger inventory and a much smaller sense of dignity.
responses.talk: You knock politely. Nobody answers. Rude, but expected from a door that old.
responses.default: You examine the locked door. It remains steadfastly locked, as is its right.

responses.look: A natural rock shelf near the cave entrance. Damp and covered in a thin layer of moss. Various objects have been abandoned here over the years by people who clearly had better things to do.
responses.take: The ledge is part of the cave wall. You'd need a pickaxe and significantly more ambition.
responses.use: You could use it as a shelf, but your standards for furniture are already low enough.
responses.default: You examine the rock ledge. It's a ledge. Made of rock. Riveting.

responses.look: A small glass bottle perched on a rock ledge. The liquid inside shifts between colors you're fairly sure shouldn't exist in nature. The label decomposed sometime during the previous dynasty.
responses.use: Drink it? Here? Without even reading the label? Well, the label decomposed centuries ago, so that's a moot point. Still, the liquid is doing things no honest liquid should.

responses.look: A mushroom growing on the cave wall, pulsing with an eerie blue bioluminescence. It's either magical, radioactive, or nature's attempt at a nightlight. Possibly all three.

actions.text: You pluck the glowing mushroom from the wall. It pulses warmly in your hand, like a nightlight for the terminally adventurous. Your pockets will smell of fungus for weeks.
You pocket the mysterious bottle. The liquid sloshes ominously. This is definitely the kind of decision that looks great in hindsight. Or terrible. One of those.

The rusty key grinds into the lock with a sound that makes your teeth ache. With a reluctant clunk, the door swings open, revealing stairs descending into darkness. The key crumbles to rust dust in your hand. One-time use, apparently. Just like your courage.
You wedge the glowing mushroom onto the stick. It holds, mostly. You've created a makeshift torch that casts an eerie blue glow. MacGyver would be... not exactly proud, but perhaps mildly amused.
You wedge the glowing mushroom onto the stick. It holds, mostly. You've created a makeshift torch that casts an eerie blue glow. MacGyver would be... not exactly proud, but perhaps mildly amused.

poison-death.title: Death by Curiosity
poison-death.narratorText: You drink the mysterious liquid. It tastes like regret, bad decisions, and something that might once have been a plum. The colors in your vision expand to include shades that don't exist, and then contract to include nothing at all. You crumple to the floor in a heap of poor judgment. The bottle rolls away, unfinished. Even it doesn't want to be associated with you anymore.

dynamicDescriptions.door-unlocked: A yawning cave mouth gapes before you. The heavy oak door now hangs open, revealing deeper passages beyond. The cold air carries a faint whisper of something below -- adventure, perhaps. Or possibly just a draft with delusions of grandeur.
